title: DORA ICT Third-Party Risk Register — Control Testing Evidence Freshness by Provider Tier

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.24 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.24 (Digital operational-resilience testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.39: DORA ICT Third-Party Risk Register — Control Testing Evidence Freshness by Provider Tier.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.39' running on index=vendor sourcetype="dd:assurance" (provider_id, tier, report_type, period_end, uploaded_at, onsite_test_date), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk DB Connect, document metadata via HEC
spl:
index=vendor sourcetype="dd:assurance" earliest=-1d
| eval uploaded=strptime(uploaded_at,"%Y-%m-%d"), period=strptime(period_end,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-period)/86400,0)
| eval breach=case(
    tier="1" AND age_days>120, 1,
    tier="2" AND age_days>270, 1,
    tier="3" AND age_days>365, 1,
    1=1, 0)
| where breach=1
| table provider_id, tier, report_type, period_end, age_days
description: Higher-tier providers need fresher assurance. Tracking SOC2/ISO report ingestion dates and on-site test completion per tier enforces differentiated diligence aligned with criticality.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vendor and sourcetypes dd:assurance. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Calibrate tier thresholds with compliance; (2) integrate Vanta/SecurityScorecard only as supplementary; (3) track `onsite_test_date` for tier 1; (4) auto-email vendor for missing reports; (5) map to RoI provider keys. Indexes required: `index=vendor`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=dd:assurance`.
visualization: Table (stale assurance), Bar by tier, Single value (providers overdue).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for higher-tier providers need fresher assurance. tracking soc2/iso report ingestion dates and on-site test completion per tier enforces differentiated diligence aligned with criticality. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect, document metadata via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vendor` `sourcetype="dd:assurance"` (provider_id, tier, report_type, period_end, uploaded_at, onsite_test_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Calibrate tier thresholds with compliance; (2) integrate Vanta/SecurityScorecard only as supplementary; (3) track `onsite_test_date` for tier 1; (4) auto-email vendor for missing reports; (5) map to RoI provider keys.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vendor sourcetype="dd:assurance" earliest=-1d
| eval uploaded=strptime(uploaded_at,"%Y-%m-%d"), period=strptime(period_end,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-period)/86400,0)
| eval breach=case(
    tier="1" AND age_days>120, 1,
    tier="2" AND age_days>270, 1,
    tier="3" AND age_days>365, 1,
    1=1, 0)
| where breach=1
| table provider_id, tier, report_type, period_end, age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA ICT Third-Party Risk Register — Control Testing Evidence Freshness by Provider Tier** — Higher-tier providers need fresher assurance. Tracking SOC2/ISO report ingestion dates and on-site test completion per tier enforces differentiated diligence aligned with criticality.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vendor` `sourcetype="dd:assurance"` (provider_id, tier, report_type, period_end, uploaded_at, onsite_test_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect, document metadata via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vendor; **sourcetype**: dd:assurance. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vendor, sourcetype="dd:assurance", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uploaded** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where breach=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_days, breach, period_end, provider_id, report_type, tier, uploaded.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale assurance), Bar by tier, Single value (providers overdue).
